title: EDHOC: Header declares c_i/c_r as _Nonnull but implementation allows NULL
description:
API MISUSE: Function declarations in header mark c_i/c_r as _Nonnull, but implementation accepts NULL for auto-generation.

Header (edhoc.h):
- Line 192: const uint8_t *_Nonnull c_i
- Line 251: const uint8_t *_Nonnull c_r

Implementation (edhoc.c):
- Lines 398-407: if (c_i != NULL && c_i_len > 0) else generate random
- Lines 791-800: if (c_r != NULL && c_r_len > 0) else generate random

The _Nonnull annotation tells callers the parameter must not be NULL, but the implementation actually handles NULL specially for auto-generation.

Impact: 
1. Callers will be warned by compiler for passing NULL
2. API contract is misleading
3. Documentation says 'copied' but NULL triggers generation

Fix: Either:
1. Change _Nonnull to _Nullable and document NULL behavior
2. Remove auto-generation and require explicit IDs

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/include/lichen/edhoc.h:192,251
close_reason: Fixed API contract by using _Nullable for c_i/c_r and updating docs to describe NULL=auto-generate random 1-byte CID behavior. Matches impl in edhoc.c. Verified with header update.
